title: Undercut-range and pit-wave interaction features
summary: Public F1 strategy analysis and recent competition-aware race-strategy papers argue pit timing is strongly driven by rival proximity, clean-air opportunity, and reaction to opponent stops, not just a car’s own tyre age.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analysis and recent competition-aware race-strategy papers argue pit timing is strongly driven by rival proximity, clean-air opportunity, and reaction to opponent stops, not just a car’s own tyre age.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year` + `LapNumber`, build same-lap features only: neighbors by `Position` (±1/±2), their `Compound`/`TyreLife`/`PitStop`, counts of nearby fresh-tyre cars, counts of same-lap pitters, and own `LapTime_Delta` rank versus nearby cars. Treat these as undercut/overcut and traffic proxies.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on reaction/defensive stops that current single-row tyre-window features miss.
risk: No true time-gap column exists, so position-band proxies may be noisy; any cross-lap aggregation would leak.
